summary:
Cytokines may indirectly influence epilepsy through their established role in central nervous system inflammation, which can alter neuronal excitability and increase seizure susceptibility. Although direct evidence linking cytokines to epileptogenesis is limited, biological plausibility supports their contributory role in modulating seizures via inflammatory pathways. 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant aspects related to the query about cytokines' function in epilepsy.
